prompt.content:
I want to perform a simple market basket analysis on device trade‑ins to uncover brand‑and‑model co‑occurrence patterns: specifically, which trade‑in device patterns most often correspond to particular purchased brand/models, focusing this analysis on identifiable brands/models within the data.
Use the tables device_purchases and devices. Join on device_id for the purchased device, and enrich trade_in_device with manufacturer by fuzzy matching against devices.model_name (case‑insensitive substring match). If a match is found, prepend the manufacturer to the original trade‑in text using a single space; if no match is found, keep the trade‑in text unchanged. Treat the enriched trade_in_device as the antecedent and the purchased device (manufacturer || ' ' || model_name) as the consequent. Compute pair counts, support, confidence, lift, and leverage. For rules where the consequent is “Apple iPhone 15 Plus,” return the highest‑lift rule; break ties by higher support, then higher confidence. Report the the antecedent, pair counts, support, confidence, lift, and leverage for this rule. Round support, confidence, lift, and leverage to four decimals. Perform the analysis using SQLite. Provide the SQL script and results.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
trade_in_device               purchased_device       pair_cnt         confidence_trade_to_purchased     support          lift           leverage
Apple iPhone 12              Apple iPhone 15 Plus         4                         0.0261                                       0.0049       1.4292        0.0015

```
-- Market basket analysis: trade-in device -> purchased device
WITH trade_in_raw AS (
  -- 1) Collect distinct, non-generic trade-in strings
  SELECT DISTINCT TRIM(trade_in_device) AS trade_in_device
  FROM device_purchases
  WHERE trade_in_device IS NOT NULL
    AND TRIM(trade_in_device) <> ''
    AND TRIM(trade_in_device) <> 'Old device'
),
trade_in_candidates AS (
  -- 2) Fuzzy match trade-ins to device models (first word) and rank by longest model name
  SELECT
    r.trade_in_device,
    d.manufacturer,
    d.model_name,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
      PARTITION BY r.trade_in_device
      ORDER BY LENGTH(d.model_name) DESC
    ) AS rn
  FROM trade_in_raw r
  JOIN devices d
    ON instr(
         LOWER(r.trade_in_device),
         LOWER(
           CASE
             WHEN instr(d.model_name, ' ') > 0
               THEN substr(d.model_name, 1, instr(d.model_name, ' ') - 1)
             ELSE d.model_name
           END
         )
       ) > 0
),
trade_in_match AS (
  -- 3) Keep the best match per trade-in string
  SELECT trade_in_device, manufacturer, model_name
  FROM trade_in_candidates
  WHERE rn = 1
),
base AS (
  -- 4) Build enriched trade-in (prepend manufacturer) and purchased device label
  SELECT
    p.purchase_id,
    CASE
      WHEN m.manufacturer IS NOT NULL
        THEN m.manufacturer || ' ' || TRIM(p.trade_in_device)
      ELSE TRIM(p.trade_in_device)
    END AS trade_in_device,
    d.manufacturer || ' ' || d.model_name AS purchased_device
  FROM device_purchases p
  JOIN devices d ON d.device_id = p.device_id
  LEFT JOIN trade_in_match m
    ON m.trade_in_device = TRIM(p.trade_in_device)
  WHERE p.trade_in_device IS NOT NULL
    AND TRIM(p.trade_in_device) <> ''
    AND TRIM(p.trade_in_device) <> 'Old device'
),
counts AS (
  -- 5) Count trade-in -> purchased device pairs
  SELECT trade_in_device, purchased_device, COUNT(*) AS pair_cnt
  FROM base
  GROUP BY trade_in_device, purchased_device
),
trade_totals AS (
  -- 6) Count total occurrences per trade-in (antecedent)
  SELECT trade_in_device, COUNT(*) AS trade_cnt
  FROM base
  GROUP BY trade_in_device
),
purch_totals AS (
  -- 7) Count total occurrences per purchased device (consequent)
  SELECT purchased_device, COUNT(*) AS purch_cnt
  FROM base
  GROUP BY purchased_device
),
all_totals AS (
  -- 8) Count total transactions for support calculations
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_cnt FROM base
)
SELECT
  c.trade_in_device,
  c.purchased_device,
  c.pair_cnt,
  ROUND(1.0 * c.pair_cnt / t.trade_cnt, 4) AS confidence_trade_to_purchased,
  ROUND(1.0 * c.pair_cnt / a.total_cnt, 4) AS support,
  ROUND((1.0 * c.pair_cnt / t.trade_cnt) / (1.0 * p.purch_cnt / a.total_cnt), 4) AS lift,
  ROUND(
    (1.0 * c.pair_cnt / a.total_cnt) -
    ((1.0 * t.trade_cnt / a.total_cnt) * (1.0 * p.purch_cnt / a.total_cnt)),
    4
  ) AS leverage
FROM counts c
JOIN trade_totals t ON t.trade_in_device = c.trade_in_device
JOIN purch_totals p ON p.purchased_device = c.purchased_device
JOIN all_totals a
WHERE c.purchased_device = 'Apple iPhone 15 Plus'
ORDER BY lift DESC, support DESC, confidence_trade_to_purchased DESC
LIMIT 1;
```
